findings.description: The correctness argument states that variables set to true were forced by implications starting from 'unconditionally-true clauses (empty-premise implications)' and that any satisfying assignment σ must also set those variables to true — but this is only true for variables directly forced by empty-premise clauses; variables forced by chains of implications from those roots are not necessarily true in every satisfying assignment (a satisfying assignment could set some intermediate or downstream variable to true for a different reason, or the chain argument needs to be stated inductively). More critically, the argument as written claims σ 'must also set those variables to true' without establishing the inductive step that σ must satisfy each link in the implication chain, making the proof sketch incomplete and potentially misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise to state the argument inductively: the base case is that empty-premise clauses force a variable true in every satisfying assignment; the inductive step is that if all premises of an implication are forced true in every satisfying assignment, the head must also be true in every satisfying assignment — therefore the greedy assignment is a subset of every satisfying assignment.

findings.description: The block uses 'CNF formula', 'literal', 'clause', and 'SAT' as foundational concepts without introduction, but the only listed prerequisite is interval scheduling — a learner with only that background would not know these propositional-logic terms.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief definitional sentence for literal, clause, and CNF before using them, or add propositional logic / Boolean satisfiability as a prerequisite.

findings.description: The display-math block (id=54fe1019) listing the Horn formula is immediately followed by a paragraph (id=8b34da28) that explicitly interprets specific clauses in that formula, but both blocks share group_id 'worked-example-group' while the step-by-step trace blocks (ids 405526e7 onward) that walk through the computation of that exact formula are outside the group, breaking the coupling.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the 'worked-example-group' group_id to cover all numbered trace blocks (405526e7 through 73b697c6) and the concluding callout (148156f6) so the full worked example is grouped together.
